title: The Surprising Benefits of Enjoying Solitude
subtitle: 独处不是孤独：一个人待着的意外好处

paragraphs.en: I can be a reluctant socialiser. I'm sometimes secretly pleased when social plans are called off. I get restless a few hours into a hangout. I even once went on a free 10-day silent meditation retreat — not for the meditation, but for the silence. So I can relate to author Anneli Rufus, who wrote in Party of One: The Loners' Manifesto: 'When parents on TV shows punished their kids by ordering them to go to their rooms, I was confused. I loved my room. Being there behind a locked door was a treat.'
paragraphs.zh: 我可能是个不情愿的社交者。社交计划取消时，我有时暗自高兴。聚会几小时后我便焦躁不安。我甚至参加过一次免费的十日禁语冥想静修——不是为了冥想，而是为了那份寂静。因此我能理解作家安内利·鲁弗斯在《一人派对：孤独者宣言》中写的：“电视里父母惩罚孩子让他们回房间时，我很困惑。我爱我的房间。待在锁着的门后是一种享受。”

paragraphs.en: Unsocial tendencies like these are often far from ideal. Abundant research shows the harms of social isolation, considered a serious public health problem in countries that have rapidly ageing populations. In the UK, the Royal College of General Practitioners says that loneliness has the same risk level for premature death as diabetes. Strong social connections are important for cognitive functioning, motor function and a smoothly running immune system.
paragraphs.zh: 这类不善交际的倾向往往远非理想。大量研究显示社会孤立的危害，在人口迅速老龄化的国家被视为严重的公共卫生问题。英国皇家全科医师学会指出，孤独导致过早死亡的风险与糖尿病相当。牢固的社会联系对认知功能、运动机能和免疫系统顺畅运行至关重要。

paragraphs.en: This is especially clear from cases of extreme social isolation. Examples of people kept in captivity, children kept isolated in abusive orphanages, and prisoners kept in solitary confinement all show how prolonged solitude can lead to hallucinations and other forms of mental instability. But these are severe and involuntary cases of aloneness. For those of us who just prefer plenty of alone time, emerging research suggests some good news.
paragraphs.zh: 这一点在极端社会孤立的案例中尤为明显。被囚禁者、在虐待性孤儿院中被隔离的儿童以及被单独监禁的囚犯，都表明长期独处可导致幻觉及其他形式的精神失常。但这些都是严重且非自愿的孤独情形。对于我们这些单纯偏爱大量独处时间的人来说，新兴研究带来了一些好消息。

paragraphs.en: There are upsides to being alone — for both our work lives and our emotional well-being. Solitude is a time for reflection, for thinking about things more deeply. In a world increasingly focused on speed and competitiveness, solitude is precious and something to be treasured. One key benefit of solitude is improved creativity.
paragraphs.zh: 独处对工作生活和情感健康都有好处。独处是反思之时，是更深入思考事物的时刻。在一个日益注重速度和竞争的世界里，独处弥足珍贵，值得珍视。独处的一个关键益处是提升创造力。

paragraphs.en: Gregory Feist, who focuses on the psychology of creativity at California's San Jose State University, has defined creativity as thinking or activity with two key elements: originality and usefulness. He has found that personality traits commonly associated with creativity are openness, self-confidence, and autonomy — which may include a lack of concern for social norms and a preference for being alone.
paragraphs.zh: 加州圣何塞州立大学专注创造力心理学的格雷戈里·费斯特将创造力定义为具备两个关键要素的思维或活动：原创性与实用性。他发现，通常与创造力相关的人格特质是开放性、自信和自主——其中可能包括不关心社会规范和偏好独处。

paragraphs.en: In fact, Feist's research on both artists and scientists shows that one of the most prominent features of creative folks is their lesser interest in socialising. One reason for this is that such people are likely to spend sustained time alone working on their craft. Plus, Feist says, many artists are trying to make sense of their internal world through their art. Solitude allows for the reflection and observation necessary for that creative process.
paragraphs.zh: 事实上，费斯特对艺术家和科学家的研究表明，富有创造力者最显著的特征之一是对社交兴趣较低。原因之一是这类人可能长时间独自打磨技艺。此外费斯特说，许多艺术家正试图通过艺术理解内心世界并赋予其表达和意义。独处为创作过程所需的反思与观察提供了空间。

paragraphs.en: A recent justification of these ideas came from University at Buffalo psychologist Julie Bowker, who researches social withdrawal. Social withdrawal is usually categorised into three types: shyness caused by fear or anxiety; avoidance, from a dislike of socialising; and unsociability, from a preference for solitude. A paper by Bowker and her colleagues was the first to show that a type of social withdrawal could have a positive effect — they found that creativity was linked specifically to unsociability.
paragraphs.zh: 布法罗大学心理学家朱莉·鲍克研究社交退缩，为上述观点提供了新近佐证。社交退缩通常分为三类：因恐惧或焦虑导致的害羞；因厌恶社交导致的回避；以及因偏爱独处导致的不合群。鲍克及其同事的论文首次表明某种社交退缩具有积极效应——他们发现创造力与不合群特质密切相关。

paragraphs.en: They also found that unsociability had no correlation with aggression. This was significant because while previous research had suggested that unsociability might be harmless, Bowker and colleagues' paper showed that it could actually be beneficial. Unsociable people are likely to be having just enough interaction, Bowker says. They have a preference for being alone, but they also don't mind being with others.
paragraphs.zh: 他们还发现不合群与攻击性无关。这一点意义重大，因为此前研究虽暗示不合群可能无害，但鲍克团队的论文表明它实际上可能有益。鲍克说，不合群者可能只进行恰到好处的社交互动。他们偏爱独处，但也不介意与他人相处。

paragraphs.en: Still, it turns out that solitude is important for more than creativity. It's commonly believed that leaders need to be sociable. But this depends, among other things, on the personalities of their employees. One 2011 study showed that in branches of a pizza chain where employees were more passive, outgoing bosses were associated with higher profits. But where employees were more active, introverted leaders were more effective.
paragraphs.zh: 然而独处的重要性不止于创造力。人们普遍认为领导者需要善于交际，但这取决于员工的个性等因素。2011年一项研究表明，在一家披萨连锁店中，员工较为被动的分店由外向型老板管理利润更高；但在员工更主动的分店，内向型领导者更为有效。

paragraphs.en: One reason for this is that introverted people are less likely to feel threatened by strong personalities and suggestions. They're also more likely to listen. Meanwhile, since ancient times, people have been aware of a link between isolation and mental focus. After all, cultures with traditions of religious hermits believe that solitude is important for enlightenment.
paragraphs.zh: 原因之一是内向者较少感受到强势个性和建议的威胁，也更善于倾听。与此同时，自古以来人们就意识到隔绝与精神专注之间存在联系。毕竟，有宗教隐士传统的文化都相信独处对开悟至关重要。

paragraphs.en: One benefit of unsociability is the brain's state of active mental rest, which goes hand-in-hand with the stillness of being alone. When another person is present, your brain can't help but pay some attention. Daydreaming in the absence of such distractions activates the brain's default-mode network. This network helps to consolidate memory and understand others' emotions.
paragraphs.zh: 不合群的一个益处是大脑处于主动心智休息状态，这与独处时的宁静相辅相成。当有他人在场时，大脑难免会分散注意。在没有此类干扰时做白日梦会激活大脑的默认模式网络。该网络有助于巩固记忆和理解他人情绪。

paragraphs.en: Giving free rein to a wandering mind not only helps with focus in the long term but strengthens your sense of both yourself and others. Paradoxically, therefore, periods of solitude actually help when it comes time to socialise once more. Susan Cain, author of Quiet, says that creativity requires sustained attention and deep focus. When we surround ourselves with others, we automatically take in their opinions. To truly chart our own path, we must be willing to isolate ourselves, at least for some time.
paragraphs.zh: 让思绪自由驰骋不仅有助于长期专注，还能增强对自我和他人的感知。因此矛盾的是，一段独处时光反而有助于再次社交。《安静》一书作者苏珊·凯恩说，创造力需要持续关注和深度专注。当我们置身人群，会不自觉地吸收他人观点。要真正走出自己的路，必须甘于独处，至少一段时间。

paragraphs.en: Still, the line between useful solitude and dangerous isolation can be blurry. Almost anything can be adaptive or maladaptive depending on how extreme it gets, Feist says. If someone cuts off all contact, this could point to an irrational neglect of social relations. But creative unsociability is a far cry from this. In fact, Feist says, there's a real danger with people who are never alone.
paragraphs.zh: 不过，有益的独处与危险的隔绝之间界限模糊。费斯特说，几乎任何事物都可能因极端程度而变得适应或适应不良。若有人切断一切联系，可能意味着对社会关系的非理性忽视。但创造性不合群与此相去甚远。费斯特说，事实上从不独处的人才有真正危险。

paragraphs.en: It's hard to be fully self-aware and relaxed unless you have occasional solitude. In addition, introverts tend to have fewer but stronger friendships — which has been linked to greater happiness. As with many things, quality reigns over quantity. Nurturing a few solid relationships without feeling the need to constantly populate your life with endless chatting ultimately may be better for you.
paragraphs.zh: 除非偶尔独处，否则很难真正自省和放松。此外，内向者往往朋友较少但情谊更深——这与更高的幸福感相关。如同许多事，质量胜于数量。用心经营几段稳固关系，而不必用无尽闲聊填满生活，最终可能对你更有利。

paragraphs.en: Thus, if your personality tends toward unsociability, you shouldn't feel the need to change. As long as you have regular social contact, you are choosing solitude rather than being forced into it, you have at least a few good friends and your solitude is good for your well-being or productivity, there's no point agonising over how to fit a square personality into a round hole.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，若你性格偏向不合群，不必觉得需要改变。只要你有定期的社交接触，独处是自主选择而非被迫，至少有几个好友且独处有益于你的身心健康或生产力，就无需为把方形性格塞进圆孔而苦恼。
